page: Microsoft Azure FX8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU operates at a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a large 260 MB L3 cache. The system is provisioned with 168.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core.

This server includes 472 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 with VNNI and BF16 support, alongside Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated mathematical and AI workloads. Cryptographic acceleration is also present via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI flags.

In benchmark testing, the FX8mds_v2 demonstrates strong single-core performance, achieving top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 25 percent. While multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations generally fall within the average range, the server excels in lightweight LLM inference, achieving top-tier text generation speeds with a small 135M model. However, performance drops to the bottom 25 percent for text generation on larger 7B models.

Given its massive memory allocation relative to its 8 vCPUs, the FX8mds_v2 offers high resource density for memory-bound applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require high memory capacity and fast single-core CPU speeds without the need for a high vCPU count. This makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, software compilation, and lightweight text processing or inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and a substantial 168.0 GB of RAM, providing an impressive 21.0 GB of memory per core. The server is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 472 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. It excels in single-core performance, scoring in the top tier for Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks, and delivers strong single-threaded compression speeds. While multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are average, its high memory density and advanced CPU instructions make it highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and lightweight text processing workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds and massive RAM density for demanding database and caching workloads.

168.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB per core memory ratio
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
